observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: 1. Management provide an explanation for the non-reclassification of the 26 completed infrastructure projects, not in conformity to COA Circular No. 2015-009; and 2. The Municipal Accountant reclassify the 26 projects from the Construction in Progress account to the appropriate Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures accounts.

We recommended the following: 1. The Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjustments of the aforementioned enumerated, monthly bank reconciliation reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account; GF-Proper JEV# 2025-03-000111; GF-Proper JEV#2025-07-00144; GF-Proper JEV# 2025-03-000111 TF-Proper JEV#2025-07-00144 JEV# 2025-03-000027 TF-Proper JEV#2025-01-000001 JEV# 2025-03-000027 TF-NGA JEV#2025-01-000001 JEV# 2025-03-000036 TF-NGA JEV#2025-01-000035 JEV# 2025-03-000036 01-000001 01-000002 TF-Kalahi-CIDDS JEV#2025-01-000001 TF-PMNP JEV#2025-03-000024. 2. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the transactions documents and reports to avoid erroneous JEV recording of financial transactions; and 3. Management regularly update the activation of their LBP WeAccessSystem to aid them in the timely preparation of complete BRS for all existing Cash in Bank Accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books and for the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their individual records with that of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended through the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer that the Accountable Officer submit a written justification as to why his/her collections were not remitted daily and were delayed between 19 to 121 calendar days. We also recommended thru the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer that all designated Collecting Officers ensure the remittance of collections and accomplishment of Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) daily and intact.

We recommended the following: 1. Management require the concerned prior cash advances are liquidated. JEV-2025-01-000035 JEV-2025-01-000021 JEV-2025-07-000414 JEV-2025-05-LBP#20 2. Henceforth, Management strictly enforce the prompt liquidation of all cash advances pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Policy Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended, that LGU Garchitorena submit through its Chief Executive, that the Municipal General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Officer, the Municipal General Services Officer, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, conduct and finish the inventory-taking of the above Welfare Goods for Distribution as of December 31, 2024 and for the succeeding semesters thereafter; and submit the corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team, on or before the deadline set – July 31 and January 31 for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended the following: 1. The Local Chief Executive, through the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer, to discontinue the direct recognition of supplies and materials in the expense accounts; and submit a written justification why the required stock cards, supplies inventory records and reports were not prepared and maintained for CY 2024, indicating a weakness in the internal control on inventories. 2. Henceforth, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer ensure that the inventories records and reports as required in the foregoing provisions of NGAS Manual for LGUs are regularly prepared and maintained.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the balances which remained outstanding/unpaid for more than two years and for which no actual claim, administrative or judicial has been filed, in order to reflect the fair and accurate account balance in the financial statements. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant verify the validity of payables aged more than two years and send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors.

We recommended the following: 1. Management, due to NGAs, Due to LGUs, and Due to GOCCs, establish the correct balances of reciprocal accounts and transfer to the unappropriated surplus of the Government; 2. Management, in coordination with the concerned offices and/or personnel, retrieve relevant supporting documents, and make appropriate actions to settle or further those validation of the balances and coordinate with the source agencies, submit the liquidation reports, return any excess fund, and draw the necessary adjusting/correcting entries to reflect the appropriate balance of the pertinent Inter-Agency Payables in the Financial Statements; 3. The Municipal Mayor, as signatory of MOA, consider designating personnel to maintain a file for the MOAs it enters into and ensure that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant were timely furnished with a copy thereof, in order that they could also maintain a separate file compiling the copy of the MOAs for every fund transfer or grant received by the Municipality for easier tracing of the tenor of the fund transfer; and 4. The Municipal Accountant maintain and prepare a detailed Schedule of Inter-Agency Payable accounts, supported with complete subsidiary ledgers per source agency, to ensure the proper monitoring of the balances of the fund transfers and/or grants.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant: 1. Reclassify the above-mentioned transactions to their appropriate accounts and fund classifications and make the necessary adjustments on the GF-Proper to ensure that the correct balance of Other Payables is accurately reflected in the financial statements; and 2. Retrieve relevant records or coordinate with concerned parties to verify the validity of the claim against the ₱313,461.03 balance.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, provide an explanation for the discrepancies between the adjusted schedule of Taxes Withheld from suppliers and traders from whom taxes were withheld and the submitted BIR Alphalist of suppliers.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant: 1. Coordinate with PhilHealth regarding the non-acceptance in the system of the payment of remittance to be made for the employee whose salary was processed late once the remittance is made in the appealed system for the employees, it cannot be refunded. 2. Facilitate the payment/remittance of the noted unremitted balances as of December 31, 2024, and follow up the case for employees whose employment status is under appeal, in the next remittance period and file a request for refund if the appointment is later nullified; and 3. Make the necessary adjustments in the Due to PhilHealth and PhilHealth Contributions accounts to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended, that, henceforth, the Management, devise a strategy to facilitate the immediate implementation of its development projects, e.g. schedule the procurement and implementation of the projects throughout the year, and consider the conduct of Early Procurement Activities (GPPB Resolution No. 14-2019) for projects to be implemented in the first quarter of the ensuing year.

We recommended that: 1. The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Human Resource Officer to formulate a policy on the grant of BAMS-exemption; and 2. Management require all LGU personnel to record their daily attendance in the BAMS, as practicable, to improve the LGU’s human resource management.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal General Services Officer and the LDRRMO, to submit to the Audit Team the copy of the Contract/Purchase Order costing ₱3,006,250.00 to avoid appropriate audit sanction.

We recommended the following: 1. Management direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the sources, utilization, and unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements; 2. Management, through the LDRRMO and LDRRM Officer, henceforth, submit to the Audit Team the monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant; and 3. The Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the LDDRMO/LDRRM Officer, maintain their respective registries and relevant documents on a monthly basis for an effective and efficient monitoring of the status of implementation and utilization of LDRRMF per PPAs.

We recommended through the GAD Focal Point Person: 1. Submit a revised GAD Accomplishment Report for the CY 2024 that is generated from the accounting records; 2. Assign specific personnel within the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to maintain backup records and provide real-time updates on the utilization of GAD fund; and 3. Provide a justification for the delay in the implementation of the GAD Office cum Crisis Intervention Center (Phase 2), and submit the Certificate of Completion of Works related to this project.

We recommended the following: 1. Management reconcile and provide the Audit Team with written justification for the pertinent deficiencies noted as to the effective and efficient utilization and implementation of the NTF-ELCAC projects from the OPAPRU Trust Fund; 2. Management submit to the Audit Team the complete and appropriate documents particularly on the Attendance and Distribution Sheets for 9 Barangays intended to benefit from the PPAs of the OPAPRU Trust Fund; and 3. Management, particularly those involved in the Planning, Monitoring, and Implementation of PPAs from National Government Trust Funds, ensure the effective and efficient utilization of the funds to achieve its intended purpose beneficial to the constituents of the municipality.

We recommended that Management: 1. Direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prepare and submit the duly approved List of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year to the SA & ATL at the beginning of the year pursuant to Item No. 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 for verification, and regularly update the same during the year in case of additional or new PPAs to be implemented in that year; 2. Promptly comply with the posting requirement thru a standard Project Signboard as designed; and 3. Submit the duly signed Monthly Monitoring Report and/or a Statement of Work Accomplishments (SWA) based on the interval of verification (% ) percentage provided for in the Table.

We recommended the following: 1. Management requires the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the related Driver’s Trip Tickets and the Requisition and Issue Voucher/Slips, if any; and 2. Management instructs the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements for fuel consumption are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management:

require the different offices/end-users in the agency to prepare and submit their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) incorporating therein all their procurement requirements for the year and have them duly evaluated and reviewed for consolidation into a comprehensive, workable and realistic Annual Procurement Plan which would reflect the details of the procurement activities that it seeks to undertake within the calendar year;

strictly follow the procurement methods and activities as stated in their Annual Procurement Plan; and

whenever practicable, consider consolidation of purchase requests for commonly used supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to promote efficiency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction:
Submission of PPMP/APP per Office and implement, whenever practicable, the prescribed procurement methods and activities as stated in the APP.


None


None
observations.reason:
For CY 2021, there was no PPMP/APP submitted ahead as basis for the consolidated procurement of the Municipality.



No Submitted APP per Office. 


For CY 2021, the Management is still following its previous practice of purchasing supplies per Office since they could not properly monitor yet the Budget per Office. Nevertheless, for CY 2021, they already consolidated the purchase of equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Accountant, GSO and Requisitioning Party/Office to follow the required flow of transactions and accomplish/update records/reports in processing purchase and requisition of supplies and materials for proper monitoring, recording, and accounting of delivered and issued/consumed/distributed inventories; and record supplies and materials deemed inventory assets as inventories first and their corresponding expense accounts once issued/consumed/distributed.
observations.managementAction: Recording of the supplies as inventory assets first before recording of expense.
observations.reason: The Management continued its previous practice of recording as outright expense the purchase of supplies once the same are transmitted to the GSO and end-user.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the MPDP alongside with GAD Focal Point System to work on the establishment of GAD database to ensure an effective and efficient implementation of GAD.
observations.managementAction: Assignment of a personnel to work on the establishment of the LGU’s GAD database. Budget will be appropriated for to implement the same.
observations.reason: The personnel tasked to construct/develop the GAD database is still working on the LGU website; GAD database shall be focused on after the LGU website has been completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management study the possibility of reviving the ice plant either to its original plan or other purposes taking into consideration economy, efficiency and effectiveness or offer for lease the plant and equipment/machineries such as generator installed inside the plant which could generate income to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution for the conversion of the infrastructure to a compost facility.
observations.reason: There are no plans to revive the Ice Plant anymore for being ammonia-type.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert all necessary efforts to trace the transactions concerning the PDAF and return the unexpended balance to the BTr this CY 2018.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with DBM on the proper action. The previous advice was to appropriate for the return of the amount.
observations.reason: Management intended to use the unutilized funds for covid-19 pandemic response per BLGF MC 017 dated November 5, 2020.

observations.recommendation:

to immediately record all book reconciling items in the books of accounts;
review and reconcile the amounts taken up in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement with the Statement of Financial Position and subsidiary ledgers; and
regularly request monthly bank statements from the depository bank to ensure timely preparation of BRS and identification of possible reconciling items which need to be recorded to present the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account to its correct balance in the financial statements.

make the necessary adjusting journal entry to recognize the depreciation expense of ₱1,078,333.78; and
ensure that PPE items acquired in the ensuing periods are subjected to depreciation by regularly monitoring the PPE accounts.

require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances through the submission of LRs, otherwise, enforce the withholding of salary, in accordance with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel and training to all personnel with unliquidated cash advances until such time previous cash advances are fully settled; and


monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation of cash advances through updated monthly aging of the same to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to immediately record all book reconciling items in the books of accounts;
Municipal Accountant to revert back to Cash in Bank – LCCA account all unissued checks at year-end to reflect the proper balance of the account; and
Municipal Treasurer to strictly require the payees/recipients of checks to sign on the “Received” portion of the logbook and to indicate the date when it was received for internal control on checks issued.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances through the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports, otherwise, enforce the withholding of salary, in accordance with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel and training to all personnel with unliquidated cash advances until such time previous cash advances are fully settled; and
monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation of cash advances through updated monthly aging of the same to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to record the herein proposed entry to (a) correct the erroneous year-end adjustment on Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income; and (b) to make the herein journal entry whenever there are advance payments on Real Property Tax, as follows:

We recommended that the Management direct:
the GSO to prepare a list of PPEs found at station and include the vehicle in the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant’s office, and consistently monitor the PPEs that may be found at station but are not included in the physical count to ensure that the PPEs of the LGU are well accounted for;
the Inventory Committee to facilitate the determination of cost/value of the vehicle to be taken up in the books; and
the Municipal Accountant to take-up the necessary accounting entries and ensure that the donated vehicle is supported with the minimum documentary requirements in accordance with rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management (a) require the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Funds to COA; and (b) strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002 particularly on the compliance on the submission of reports and recording of LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
require the different offices/end-users in the agency to prepare and submit their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) incorporating therein all their procurement requirements for the year and have them duly evaluated and reviewed for consolidation into a comprehensive, workable and realistic APP which would reflect the details of the procurement activities that it seeks to undertake within the calendar year;
strictly follow the procurement methods and activities as stated in their APP, unless it becomes impractical to use the same, when another mode of procurement may be adopted through a Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) resolution ; and
whenever practicable, consider consolidation of purchase requests for commonly used supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to promote efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
direct the HRMO to formulate policy guidelines/internal rules and procedures on the rendition of OT services of their personnel and revisit the conditions on the authority to render OT services under CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2015;
consider the use of CTO as the remuneration for OT services rendered and justify that resort to OT pay fell under exceptional cases; and
monitor the grant of OT pay and control the excessive charging against the annual budget.

We recommended that Management direct the:
direct LGU Drivers to accomplish every DTT with complete data;
require the preparation and submission of Daily Gas Issue Record, Monthly Summary of RIS, MROT for each vehicle and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and
stop the grant of gasoline reimbursements to those who use private vehicles as a mode of transportation during official travel.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
prepare the monthly utilization reports of BGCM and post the same in LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month; and
comply with the requirement of posting in the GPPB online portal, website of the LGU and at any conspicuous place in the premises of the LGU.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office: (a) submit the lacking documents and (b) ensure that all documentary requirements are complied with before release/distribution of SAP is made to avoid audit sanctions.

We recommended and Management agreed to create a well-planned LDF program to minimize changes that cause unnecessary delays and prepare catch up plan, if necessary, to promptly deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management:

direct the in-Charge of GSO and Municipal Accountant to identify lost properties and the officers/employees accountable for the lost properties and use the Loss on Assets account only if accountability was established and has required grant of relief from accountability;

follow the procedures on the disposal and derecognition of physically existing unserviceable properties per COA Circular No. 95-006 and record under the Loss on Sale of Assets (5-05-04-070) account, if the disposal of properties resulted to a loss; and

direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the improper derecognition of unserviceable properties.

observations.managementAction:
Restated in Part II of this Report. | Management resorted to Small Value Procurements in the acquisition of some supplies and equipment which could have otherwise been done through public bidding.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

Designation of a Petty Cash Custodian during CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) alongside with GAD Focal Point System to work on the establishment of GAD database to ensure an effective and efficient implementation of GAD;

direct the Municipal Budget Office, Accounting Unit and designated GAD Focal Person to coordinate and properly monitor GAD fund utilization to guide them in the timely and prompt implementation of GAD PPAs; and

propose that the designated GAD Focal Person and as deemed necessary, other employees involved in GAD implementation be able to attend necessary training and seminars on GAD to properly equipped them on the implementation of LGU’s GAD.
observations.managementAction: Management assigned a personnel to work on the establishment of the LGU’s GAD database.
observations.reason: The personnel tasked to construct/develop the GAD database is still working on the LGU website; GAD database shall be focused on after the LGU website has been completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Municipal Engineer to establish a well-defined policies or system on the monitoring of LGU’s projects and be able to prepare a catch-up plan to accomplish all projects on time;

take appropriate action to hasten the delivery of equipment/machineries and other tools needed to run the operations of the composting and agri-fishery processing facilities; and



impose necessary sanctions against erring contractors such as payment of liquidated damages or forfeiture of their security bonds as stipulated in the respective contracts and as provided also under the procurement law, RA 9184, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer has submitted a monitoring report on the subject projects with delayed implementation.

The Certificate of Completion was not issued by the LGU due to the contractor’s failure to deliver the machineries included in the project’s Program of Works (POW).


The LGU has already issued Notice of Termination to the contractor of the project on Construction of Community Fish Landing Center due to the latter’s abandonment of the project.

A Notice of Termination of Contract has been issued and the final claim of the contractor on the project on Construction of Agri-Fishery Processing Facility was also forfeited by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

create a well-planned program as guide of action in the implementation of 20 per cent LDF PPAs including set timelines for the preparation of program of works and bidding schedules; and

instruct the Municipal Engineer to 1) establish a well-defined monitoring system for all LDF projects of the LGU and involve all the personnel of the Office of the Municipal Engineer; and 2) assign to engineering personnel with strict set deadlines the preparation of program of work based on the LGU’s planned schedules of the implementation of the LDF projects to facilitate the prompt completion of projects and be able to immediately deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the public.
observations.managementAction:
Compliance with the audit recommendation.

Projects funded under the CY 2019 20 per cent LDF were already completed this CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct all concerned municipal officials to monitor the implementation of the unimplemented and on-going projects to facilitate the prompt completion thereof to immediately deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the public.
observations.managementAction: Completion of the LGU’s projects funded under the CY 2018 20 per cent LDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management give equal priority on the implementation of DRRM PPAs particularly those involving disaster preparedness and prepare complete DRRM Plan in accordance with the above-cited law.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balances will be reprogrammed this CY 2021.
observations.reason: Implementation of the LDRRMF PPAs were not completed due to the restrictions brought about by the covid-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: exert effort for the approval of the LGUs SWM Plan;  monitor the implementation of the existing programs on segregation and collection of garbage; adopt additional specific revenue-generating measure to promote the viability of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan; hasten the establishment and construction of LGU’s MRF and/or sanitary land fill as well as assistance to barangays for them to be able to establish their own MRF; and prepare and submit annual SWM Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU’s SWM Board facilitated revision of the SWM Plan per the comments/review by the EMB-DENR.

Implementation of the other SWM PPAs shall be done once adequate funding allocation and manpower has been given.
observations.reason:
Some of the barangays do not have their own SWM Board.

Insufficiency of appropriation for amendment of SWM ordinances.

IEC Materials were already printed but distribution thereof was not completed due to restrictions brought about by the covid-19 pandemic.


The LGU is still waiting for the inspection to be completed for the possible SLF site within the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

direct the Municipal Assessor to closely coordinate with the Provincial Assessor of the Province of Camarines Sur to raise the issue of general revision of assessment of real properties and passage of new SFMV with fully documented course of action on this matter; and



assign additional manpower to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, if necessary, that will focus on the account of real property owners and help in the conduct of house-to-house visit for issuance of Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real Property Tax to enforced collection of unpaid taxes.
observations.managementAction:
Management has already coordinated with the Provincial Assessor’s Office with regards to the request for the revision of the assessment of real properties and updating of the SFMV.


Letter requests have already been made for the request for additional manpower in the MTO.

Creation of five treasury positions was included in the CY 2021 AIP for funding in CY 2022.
observations.reason:
There was only a Partial Revision of SFMV. Revision was only for residential and commercial lots.

Lack of adequate equipment and supplies in the Office of the Municipal Assessor was also noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the regulations on granting of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002; and designate one or more additional special disbursing officers that will be responsible in the cash advances for other activities of the Municipality that could not anymore be handled by the present accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management study the possibility of reviving the ice plant either to its original plan or other purposes taking into consideration economy, efficiency and effectiveness or offer for lease the plant and equipment/machineries such as generator installed inside the plant which could generate income to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management discussed plans with possible investors who can either revive the ice plant operation or use the plant for other purposes.
observations.reason: Management has still no concrete plans regarding the ice plant project in the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the previous GSO to turn over PPE records and reports to the present designated GSO to expedite the updating thereof; and


require the newly designated General Services Officer to update/submit PPE records/RPCPPE promptly as required in the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Submission of updated PPE records and reports by the newly designated GSO.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented | There was no proper and complete turn-over of documents from the previous GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert all necessary efforts to trace the transactions concerning the PDAF and return the unexpended balance to the BTr this CY 2018.
observations.reason: The unexpended PDAF was not yet returned the Bureau of Treasury during CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
direct the Accountant, GSO and Requisitioning Party/Office to follow the required flow of transactions and accomplish/update records/reports in processing purchase and requisition of supplies and materials for proper monitoring, recording and accounting of delivered and issued/consumed/distributed inventories; and
record supplies and materials deemed inventory assets as inventories first and their corresponding expense accounts once issued/consumed/distributed.

We recommended that the Management:
direct the in-Charge of GSO and Municipal Accountant to identify lost properties and the officers/employees accountable for the lost properties and use the Loss on Assets account only if accountability was established and has required grant of relief from accountability;
follow the procedures on the disposal and derecognition of physically existing unserviceable properties per COA Circular No. 95-006 and record under the Loss on Sale of Assets (5-05-04-070) account, if the disposal of properties resulted to a loss; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the improper derecognition of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management:
adhere strictly with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445 at all times and submit/attach complete contract documents and required supporting documents to government disbursements;
submit immediately the subject/needed lacking supporting documents to facilitate technical review and evaluation of the completed projects; and
refrain from approving government claims with incomplete supporting documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
strictly adhere to government’s procurement law to ensure that transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the LGU’s procurement process are observed; and
establish petty cash fund, if necessary, for small amounts of expenses and limit reimbursement on necessary procurements only and ensure that all required supporting documents are submitted.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) alongside with GAD Focal Point System to work on the establishment of GAD database to ensure an effective and efficient implementation of GAD;
direct the Municipal Budget Office, Accounting Unit and designated GAD Focal Person to coordinate and properly monitor GAD fund utilization to guide them in the timely and prompt implementation of GAD PPAs; and
propose that the designated GAD Focal Person and as deemed necessary, other employees involved in GAD implementation be able to attend necessary training and seminars on GAD to properly equipped them on the implementation of LGU’s GAD.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Engineer to establish a well-defined policies or system on the monitoring of LGU’s projects and be able to prepare a catch-up plan to accomplish all projects on time;
take appropriate action to hasten the delivery of equipment/machineries and other tools needed to run the operations of the composting and agri-fishery processing facilities; and
impose necessary sanctions against erring contractors such as payment of liquidated damages or forfeiture of their security bonds as stipulated in the respective contracts and as provided also under the procurement law, RA 9184, if warranted.

We recommended that the Management:
create a well-planned program as guide of action in the implementation of 20 per cent LDF PPAs including set timelines for the preparation of program of works and bidding schedules; and
instruct the Municipal Engineer to 1) establish a well-defined monitoring system for all LDF projects of the LGU and involve all the personnel of the Office of the Municipal Engineer; and 2) assign to engineering personnel with strict set deadlines the preparation of program of work based on the LGU’s planned schedules of the implementation of the LDF projects to facilitate the prompt completion of projects and be able to immediately deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the public.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury account and to bring the account to its correct balance.
observations.managementAction: Journal entries were posted in CY 2019 to correct the balance of the Cash-Local Treasury account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE records/report of the missing properties with GSO and establish the accountability on the missing firefighting equipment acquired in CY 2014 since the subject properties could still be considered new and valuable as compared to the missing fully depreciated motor vehicles; and file necessary administrative or any applicable case, if deemed warranted, against the erring accountable officer; and



require the Municipal Accountant to derecognized from the books the carrying value of the missing properties to correct the value of LGU’s assets/properties.

The entry made for the derecognition of LGU’s assets/properties did not conform with the applicable accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

Please refer to Part II of this Report. | None

The carrying value of noted missing movable properties were derecognized from the books during CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to draw appropriate Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the recording in the books of accounts of the LGU and present the Due to NGAs account to its correct balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An adjusting journal entry was recorded during CY 2019 to bring the Due to NGAs and cash accounts to their correct balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

exert effort in reconciling the balances of reciprocal accounts and maintain SLs for receivable and payable accounts as well as other assets and expense accounts and regularly reconcile the amount with GL balances and update recording/posting of transactions at all times; and

conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to identify possible errors/adjustments which need to be recorded in the books.

Subsidiary Ledgers for the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts were already being maintained.


Valid reconciling items and adjustments were recorded during CY 2019 to reconcile the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

instruct all concerned municipal officials to monitor the implementation of the unimplemented and on-going projects to facilitate the prompt completion thereof to immediately deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the public; and



create a well-planned program of implementation of 20 per cent LDF PPAs including set timelines for the preparation of program of works and bidding schedules so that delays in the implementation of LDF projects are avoided.

 require the Municipal Assessor to conduct periodic ocular inspection of real properties within the LGU’s jurisdiction to appraise and assess newly constructed and occupied properties by virtue of Section 204 “Declaration of Real Property by the Assessor” of RA 7160 as well as the taxable improvements to properties in order to update tax levied and collected from taxpayers; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances thru the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports. Otherwise, withholding salary due them is proper, in accordance with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002;

stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel to all personnel with unliquidated cash advance until such time previous cash advances are fully settled; and

monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation of cash advances through updated monthly aging of the same to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We ~ recommended  that Management furnish the Audit Team Notice of Invitation to Bid in ensuing procurement process t0 enhance transparency and to avoid any adverse sanctions in the future
We  recommended that Management comply withthe provisions on Section 325(a) of RA 7160 in ensuing years 1o avoid adverse sanctions in the future,
We  recommended  that Management apply for the correct amount of bond to cover the cash accountabifity of their accountable officers.

We  recommended that Management provide the oversight agencies copies of the approved Annual GAD Plan and Budger and the prescribed  Accomplishment Report on GAD and its supporting  documents  to comply with the requirements of the law.

We recommended  that Management  require  the Municipal  Treasurer  to undertake strategies  to improve the efficiency rating on the collection of Real Property Taxes to augment existing funds for priority programs,  activities  and projects of the Local Government Unit.
